paper_title: Environmental and Biological Data Analysis in Upper Paraná River Floodplain

methods.description: This method performs a Principal Components Analysis to reduce the dimensionality of environmental data, facilitating the interpretation of results and identification of spatial patterns.

steps.description: Perform PCA using PC-Ord 5.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA using PC-Ord 5.0

steps.description: Test the significance of the PCA axes using ANOVA.
steps.searchable_summary: test PCA axes significance with ANOVA

steps.description: Graphically represent the PCA results using Statistica 7.1.
steps.searchable_summary: create PCA result graphs with Statistica 7.1

methods.description: This method calculates the coefficient of variation for each lake to assess environmental heterogeneity, considering physical, chemical, and biological characteristics.

steps.description: Calculate the coefficient of variation for each lake.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate coefficient of variation for lakes

methods.description: This method calculates the Biodiversity Score (BS) by summing the Rarity Index of morphotypes in different environments.

steps.description: Calculate the Rarity Index for each species using Equation 3.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Rarity Index for species

steps.description: Sum the Rarity Index values to obtain the Biodiversity Score for each environment using Equation 2.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Biodiversity Score using Rarity Index

steps.description: Correct the Biodiversity Score values using Equation 4.
steps.searchable_summary: correct Biodiversity Score values

methods.description: This method detects the level of nestedness between Chironomidae communities using the NODF index.

steps.description: Build an incidence matrix based on the ordering of environments into columns and species richness in rows.
steps.searchable_summary: build incidence matrix for nestedness analysis

steps.description: Perform nestedness analysis using the vegan package in R.

methods.description: This method performs ANOVA to identify patterns for the gradient of environmental heterogeneity and biological attributes.

steps.description: Perform one-way ANOVA considering spatial and temporal variations.

methods.description: This method uses Spearman's correlations to assess relationships between environmental heterogeneity and Chironomidae community attributes.

methods.description: This method describes the sampling process for biological and abiotic data collection in the Upper Paraná River floodplain.

steps.description: Perform quarterly sampling at 29 floodplain lakes from March to December 2010.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct quarterly sampling at floodplain lakes

steps.description: Take samples at three sites along transects at each station.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples along transects

steps.description: Analyze water samples for temperature, dissolved oxygen, pH, turbidity, and conductivity.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water samples for various parameters

steps.description: Determine chlorophyll-a, nitrate, and orthophosphate concentrations from water samples.
steps.searchable_summary: determine concentrations of chlorophyll-a, nitrate, and orthophosphate

steps.description: Wash sediment samples in sequential sieves and fix retained sediment in alcohol.
steps.searchable_summary: process sediment samples with sieves and fix in alcohol

steps.description: Dissect and mount Chironomidae larvae on slides for identification.

steps.description: Identify Chironomidae larvae to the lowest possible taxonomic level.

steps.description: Determine riparian vegetation cover using a gridded mirror.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate riparian vegetation cover with gridded mirror

steps.description: Determine granulometric texture using modified Wentworth methodology.
